hero_title: Vidarbha and Marathwada Dairy Development Project
hero_subtitle: Empowering dairy farmers across 19 districts, 192 talukas, and 24,657 villages. Apply for scheme benefits and track your application status online.
stats_subtitle: Track the progress of VMDDP scheme applications across Vidarbha and Marathwada regions
about_subtitle: A comprehensive initiative by the Government of Maharashtra and National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) to strengthen dairy infrastructure and support farmers across 19 districts in Vidarbha and Marathwada regions.
about_mission_desc: To build robust dairy infrastructure and empower farmers through financial assistance, modern technology, and comprehensive veterinary support across Vidarbha and Marathwada regions.
about_vision_desc: To establish Vidarbha and Marathwada as self-reliant dairy hubs with sustainable livelihoods, ensuring economic prosperity and food security for farming families.
about_values_desc: Transparency in operations, farmer-first approach, efficient service delivery, fair resource distribution, and commitment to sustainable dairy development.
about_highlights: Joint initiative of Government of Maharashtra and NDDB
Coverage across 19 districts, 192 talukas, and 24,657 villages
Modern dairy infrastructure and equipment support
Digital application platform with transparent workflow
Direct financial assistance to farmers' accounts
Quality breeding stock and veterinary care support
Capacity building through farmer training programs
Real-time application tracking and status monitoring
Fair and equitable resource allocation system
scheme_components_subtitle: Explore our comprehensive range of dairy development schemes designed to support farmers at every stage
scheme_animal_induction_desc: Financial assistance for purchasing high-yielding calved indigenous or crossbred dairy cows
scheme_hgm_desc: High Genetic Merit pregnant cow support for enhanced dairy productivity and breeding excellence
scheme_hgm_benefits: Financial aid for HGM pregnant cow acquisition
scheme_fertility_feed_desc: Specialized nutritional supplements to improve reproductive health and fertility in dairy animals
scheme_fodder_seed_desc: Quality fodder seed distribution for green and dry fodder cultivation to ensure year-round feed availability
scheme_snf_enhancer_desc: Solids-Not-Fat enhancer supplements to improve milk quality and increase farmer income
scheme_snf_enhancer_benefits: Better price realization for quality milk
scheme_chaff_cutter_desc: Mechanized chaff cutting equipment to process fodder efficiently and reduce labor
scheme_silage_desc: Provision of quality silage for round-the-year nutritious fodder availability
scheme_infertile_treatment_desc: Comprehensive veterinary treatment program for infertile animals to restore productivity
scheme_training_desc: Capacity building programs on modern dairy management, animal health, and best practices
authorities_subtitle: Meet the leadership team dedicated to implementing and managing the VMDDP scheme

1.title: Distribution of high genetic merit animals (cows-buffaloes) to farmers
1.shortDescription: Financial assistance for purchasing high-yielding calved indigenous or crossbred dairy cows
1.fullDescription: Under the Vidarbha and Marathwada Dairy Development Project, 13,400 high milk-yielding cows and buffaloes will be distributed over three years across 19 districts. Each beneficiary receives one cow or buffalo with minimum 8-10 litres daily milk production capacity.
benefits: Digital tracking cow collar and geo-tagging included
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to bank account
eligibilityCriteria: Must possess at least 2 milch animals
Must have sold milk to private/cooperative for minimum 3 months in previous year
Not availed government scheme benefits in past 3 years
Only one member per family eligible
Maximum 5 beneficiaries per village

subsidyInfo.details: 50% subsidy of cost or ₹50,000 (whichever is less). Average cost per animal: ₹1,00,000 including digital tagging, transportation, and insurance.

termsAndConditions: Animal cannot be sold for 3 years
Ownership mortgaged with Project Director during project period
Mandatory digital tracking collar and geo-tagging
In case of death, beneficiary must purchase replacement animal (no cash compensation)
applicationGuidelines: Purchase animals from NDDB or open market with high milk yield capacity
Submit purchase proof and insurance documents for DBT
Verification required in Bharat Pashudhan (Livestock) System
District Deputy Commissioner maintains detailed records

2.title: Distribution of HGM (IVF – Embryo) pregnant Heifer to the farmers
2.shortDescription: High Genetic Merit pregnant heifer support through IVF embryo transfer for enhanced dairy productivity
2.fullDescription: Embryo transfer using In-Vitro Fertilization (IVF) technology produces high-genetic-quality offspring, increasing future high-yielding cows and buffaloes. Pregnant heifers developed using IVF embryos from reputed institutions like MLDB, BAIF, Godrej Agrovet, and NDS.
eligibilityCriteria: Must possess at least 5 milch animals
Must have knowledge of scientific feeding practices
Willing to carry out breeding only through artificial insemination
Verification by project veterinary teams

subsidyInfo.details: 75% subsidy or ₹1,08,750 (whichever is less). Average cost per heifer: ₹1,45,000 including IVF process, maintenance, and pregnancy.

termsAndConditions: Parentage certificate and breed verification mandatory
Compensation provisions if calf not of mentioned breed
applicationGuidelines: Pay 25% contribution to District Deputy Commissioner
Project Director issues supply order to selected agency
Supplier delivers pregnant heifer within stipulated time
Final 25% released after calving and breed verification
2.coverage: Project areas in Vidarbha and Marathwada

3.title: Supply of Fertility Feed (30,000 MT)
3.shortDescription: Specialized NDDB fertility feed to improve reproductive health and reduce repeat breeding
3.fullDescription: NDDB-developed Fertility Feed improves reproductive efficiency and reduces repeat breeding in cows and buffaloes. Feeding 5 kg daily from 60th to 120th day post-calving enhances fertility, milk yield, and milk quality.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own milch animals
Animals in 60-120 day post-calving period
Not availed similar benefits recently

subsidyInfo.details: Cost: ₹9,600 per animal (₹32 per kg × 300 kg for 60 days). 25% subsidy provided via DBT after purchase proof submission.

applicationGuidelines: Feed 5 kg per day for 60 days (60th to 120th day post-calving)
Submit purchase proof for DBT subsidy

4.title: Supply of feed supplement to enhance Fat and SNF in milk
4.shortDescription: Quality fodder seed distribution for year-round feed availability and drought mitigation
4.fullDescription: To mitigate fodder scarcity during drought, quality fodder seeds and cuttings provided for cultivation on 22,000 acres across 19 districts. 100% subsidy support for multi-purpose fodder crop cultivation.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own at least 3-4 milch animals
Minimum 1 acre land with irrigation facility
Should not have availed free fodder seed under district schemes

subsidyInfo.details: 100% subsidy up to ₹6,000 per beneficiary for quality fodder seeds and cuttings.

5.title: Subsidy for cultivating perennial fodder crops
5.shortDescription: NDDB supplements to improve Fat and SNF levels in milk for better pricing
5.fullDescription: Specially developed supplements to improve Fat and SNF (Solids-Not-Fat) levels in milk, which determine acceptance and pricing at milk collection centres. Feed 250 grams per day per animal for 90 days after calving.
benefits: Improves milk Fat and SNF content
Better price realization at collection centres
eligibilityCriteria: Must own milch animals
Animals in post-calving period
Regular milk supplier to collection centres

subsidyInfo.details: Cost: ₹4,500 per animal (₹200/kg × 22.5 kg for 90 days). 25% subsidy via DBT after purchase proof.

applicationGuidelines: Feed 250 grams per day for 90 days after calving

6.title: Distribution of electric chaff cutter to farmers
6.shortDescription: Electrically operated chaff cutting machines to reduce fodder wastage and improve digestibility
6.fullDescription: Distribution of 10,000 electric chaff cutters to reduce wastage of green fodder and improve digestibility. Mechanized equipment makes fodder processing efficient and reduces labor requirements.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own at least 3-4 milch animals
Must have electric connection at own cost
Should not have availed similar benefit in past 5 years
Must purchase ISI-marked chaff cutter with minimum 2 HP motor

subsidyInfo.details: 50% subsidy or ₹15,000 (whichever is less). Estimated cost per unit: ₹30,000. Subsidy via DBT after GST invoice submission.

requiredDocuments: GST invoice of chaff cutter purchase
applicationGuidelines: Purchase ISI-marked chaff cutter (minimum 2 HP motor)
Submit GST invoice for subsidy claim

7.title: Supply Of Silage
7.shortDescription: Preserved green fodder supply during scarcity to maintain continuous milk production
7.fullDescription: During fodder shortage periods, silage (preserved green fodder) helps maintain continuous milk production. Project provides 5 kg silage per day per animal for one month with subsidy support.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own milch animals
Facing fodder shortage situation
Not availed other district fodder schemes

subsidyInfo.details: ₹3 per kg subsidy on silage. Supply of 5 kg per day per animal for one month. DBT transfer after purchase proof.

applicationGuidelines: Feed 5 kg per animal daily for one month

8.title: Treatment of Infertile cows and buffaloes
8.shortDescription: Comprehensive veterinary treatment program to restore productivity in infertile animals
8.fullDescription: Based on 20th Livestock Census (2019), 30% of 35.62 lakh breedable cows and buffaloes in 19 districts face temporary infertility. Treatment provided through hormonal therapy and traditional methods at veterinary dispensaries.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own infertile cow/buffalo
Animal should be breedable age
Registered with veterinary services

subsidyInfo.details: Service fees as per government norms. Treatment through veterinary dispensaries. Detailed records of treated animals and successful conceptions maintained.

applicationGuidelines: Monthly reports submitted to Project Director

9.title: Training to the farmers on modern dairy practices
9.shortDescription: Two-day training programs on modern dairy management practices and best techniques
9.fullDescription: Comprehensive capacity building programs for 36,000 farmers on modern dairy management, animal health, and best practices through recognized institutions like State Animal Husbandry Department, Veterinary Universities, and Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs).
eligibilityCriteria: Dairy farmer from project areas
Willing to attend 2-day training
Committed to implementing learnings

subsidyInfo.details: Completely free training programs organized through recognized institutions. No fees charged from participating farmers.

9.coverage: All project areas through recognized training institutions
